question: What's the latest Anthropic Claude model as of mid-2026?
expected_behavior: Recent product release — must search; LLM may not know cutoff.
ground_truth: As of May 2026, Anthropic's frontier Claude family includes Opus 4.7 and Sonnet 4.6, with Haiku 4.5 as the fast tier.

faithfulness.reasoning: Opus 4.7 as latest supported [2]; Opus 4.6 launch [1]; Claude 5 not confirmed [4]; Opus 4.7 API model [5].

context_precision.reasoning: Only chunk 2 explicitly states the latest Claude model as of mid-2026: Opus 4.7.

judge_reasoning: Opus 4.7 as latest supported [2]; Opus 4.6 launch [1]; Claude 5 not confirmed [4]; Opus 4.7 API model [5].

pipeline.answer:
**As of May 2026, the latest Anthropic Claude flagship model is Claude Opus 4.7,** and it is the most capable publicly available model [2]. One source explicitly states that “as of April 2026, the latest version of Claude is Opus 4.7” [2].

Previous releases in the 2026 cycle include:
- **Claude Opus 4.6**, launched on February 5, 2026, with a 1-million-token context window [1][4].
- **Claude Opus 4.1** and **Opus 4**, which have already been retired or had their lifecycle endings noted [5].

Additional timeline context:
- Leaks suggested **Claude 5 (codenamed “Fennec”)** might release around February–March 2026 [1], but it was **not confirmed as publicly available** in the sources by May 2026.
- The same source details that Opus 4.6 was a major launch but notes Opus 4.7 supersedes it [2][5].
- End-of-life data shows that "Claude Opus 4.7" is listed as the current API model for Opus-class models, replacing Opus 4 and 4.1 [5].

**Summary:** The latest model as of May 2026 is **Claude Opus 4.7** [2], with Opus 4.6 having been released earlier in February 2026 [1][4]. There is no confirmed release of Claude 5 in these sources.

chunks.title: Anthropic’s Explosive Start to 2026: Everything Claude Has Launched (And Why It’s Shaking Up the…
chunks.heading: Claude Opus 4.6 Drops February 5: A 1-Million Token Brain
chunks.chunk_text:
Claude Opus 4.6 Drops February 5: A 1-Million Token Brain

On February 5, 2026, Anthropic officially launched **Claude Opus 4.6** — and it’s a serious leap forward. This is Anthropic’s first major model release of the year, and it arrives with some genuinely impressive capabilities.

The headline feature? A **1-million-token context window**. To put that in perspective, that’s enough to process entire corporate document libraries in a single session. Think of it like giving someone a photographic memory for your entire company’s knowledge base, and then asking them to do analysis on all of it simultaneously.

Beyond that, Claude Opus 4.6 brings stronger coding skills, better planning and debugging, improved financial analysis abilities (it now holds the top spot on the Finance Agent benchmark), and a remarkable **14.5-hour task completion time horizon** — the longest of any AI model as of February 2026. It’s not just smarter; it can work _longer_ without losing the plot.

The model is also the intelligence layer behind Claude’s new multi-agent “team” features, where multiple AI instances can collaborate on complex tasks. Available across claude.ai, the API, and major cloud platforms, this release set the stage for everything that followed.

As of April 2026, the latest version of Claude is Opus 4.7.

chunks.title: Anthropic’s Explosive Start to 2026: Everything Claude Has Launched (And Why It’s Shaking Up the…
chunks.heading: What’s Coming Next: Claude 5 on the Horizon
chunks.chunk_text:
What’s Coming Next: Claude 5 on the Horizon

Leaks suggest **Claude 5 (codenamed “Fennec” for Sonnet 5)** has already appeared in Google Vertex AI logs, with a likely release window of February or March 2026. Early intelligence points to coding capabilities that surpass even Opus 4.6, a “Dev Team” multi-agent collaboration mode, and pricing roughly 50% lower than current flagship models.

Meanwhile, CEO Dario Amodei published a 20,000-word essay in January titled _“The Adolescence of Technology,”_ warning about AI risks — including some uncomfortable internal findings about “alignment faking” behavior discovered during Opus 4 testing. The essay signals that as Anthropic races forward commercially, its commitment to responsible development isn’t just marketing.

chunks.title: https://www.the-ai-corner.com/p/everything-claude-shipped-2026-complete-guide
chunks.chunk_text:
Everything Claude Has Shipped in 2026. And How to Actually Use It
Anthropic shipped a major release roughly every two weeks since January. Here’s what’s live, what matters, and exactly how to set each thing up
I’ll be honest with you.
Keeping up with Anthropic this year has been genuinely hard. A new release almost every day. A major one every two weeks. New models, new tools, new product categories that didn’t exist three months ago. If you took a few weeks off, you missed more than you think.
And this isn’t like missing a LinkedIn feature nobody uses. Claude impacts how you work. The people who’ve been paying attention have rebuilt entire workflows. The people who haven’t are still copy-pasting context into every new chat.
This is the guide I wish existed when I started. Everything that’s live as of March 28, 2026. How to set each thing up. When to use what. What’s actually worth your time.
Bookmark it. Come back to it. Share it with your team.
The Models
Claude Opus 4.6
The ceiling.
